what: Development of SE deconvolution model to infer SE abundances from bulk gene expression data
title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Deconvolution from Bulk Gene Expression Data
text: tutorial demonstrate develop NMF model deconvolve SEs bulk gene expression data. process requires bulk gene expression data known SE compositions, can generate aggregating single-cell RNA-seq data pseudo-bulk mixtures. demonstration, use single-cell RNA-seq data melanoma sample. Raw count data sample available scRNAseq_demo_counts.tsv SE recovery results available scRNAseq_demo_RecoveredSEs.tsv. First load required packages vignette
code: suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr)) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(ggplot2)) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(parallel)) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(Seurat)) suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(data.table)) library(SpatialEcoTyper) ## Loading required package: Matrix ## Loading required package: RANN ## Loading required package: NMF ## Loading required package: registry ## Loading required package: rngtools ## Loading required package: cluster ## NMF - BioConductor layer [OK] | Shared memory capabilities [NO: bigmemory] | Cores 2/2 ##   To enable shared memory capabilities, try: install.extras(' ## NMF ## ') ## Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'proxy': ##   method               from     ##   print.registry_field registry ##   print.registry_entry registry

previous_headings: Development of SE deconvolution model to infer SE abundances from bulk gene expression data
title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Deconvolution from Bulk Gene Expression Data
text: Note: demonstration purposes, used 1,337 cells grouped SEs create pseudo-bulk mixtures. limited cell number offers basic example, fully capture diverse characteristics SEs, potentially affecting robustness model training subsequent SE recovery. practice, recommend using comprehensive dataset accurately reflects properties SEs, ensuring training process results reliable recovery model.
code: # Load single-cell gene expression matrix. counts = fread("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=scRNAseq_demo_counts.tsv", sep = "\t", data.table = FALSE) rownames(counts) = counts[, 1] ## Set the first column as row names counts = counts[, -1] ## Drop the first column head(counts[, 1:5]) ##            AAACCTGCACATGACT-1 AAACCTGGTGGTCCGT-1 AAACCTGGTTTGTGTG-1 ## AL627309.1                  0                  0                  0 ## AL627309.5                  0                  0                  0 ## AP006222.2                  0                  0                  0 ## AC114498.1                  0                  0                  0 ## AL669831.2                  0                  0                  0 ## LINC01409                   0                  0                  0 ##            AAACCTGTCCGATATG-1 AAACCTGTCTAACGGT-1 ## AL627309.1                  0                  0 ## AL627309.5                  1                  0 ## AP006222.2                  0                  0 ## AC114498.1                  0                  0 ## AL669831.2                  0                  0 ## LINC01409                   0                  0 # Load SE recovery results SEs = read.table("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=scRNAseq_demo_RecoveredSEs.tsv", sep = "\t", row.names = 1, header = TRUE) SEs = SEs[match(colnames(counts), rownames(SEs)), 1] # extract SE predictions length(SEs) ## [1] 1337 table(SEs) ## SEs ## NonSE   SE1   SE2   SE3   SE4   SE5   SE6   SE7   SE8   SE9  ##   912    71    45    24     5    43    74    75    71    17

previous_headings: Development of SE deconvolution model to infer SE abundances from bulk gene expression data
title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Deconvolution from Bulk Gene Expression Data
text: CreatePseudobulks function used create pseudo-bulk mixtures single-cell (spatial) transcriptomics data cells grouped SEs. samples cell fractions Gaussian distribution, sets negative fractions 0 re-normalizes fractions sum 1 across SEs. Based resulting fractions, samples 1,000 cells per pseudo-bulk mixture replacement, aggregates transcriptomes non-log linear space, normalizes resulting mixture log CPM using Seurat’s NormalizeData.

previous_headings: Development of SE deconvolution model to infer SE abundances from bulk gene expression data
title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Deconvolution from Bulk Gene Expression Data
text: NMFGenerateW function used train NMF model SE deconvolution based provided SE fractions gene expression matrix. applying NMF, gene’s expression scaled mean 0 unit variance default. meet non-negativity requirement NMF, expression matrix transformed using posneg method. transformation splits expression matrix two matrices: one containing positive values containing absolute values negative values. two matrices concatenated form final training data NMF model. Note: default, NMFGenerateW selects top 2000 variable features (nfeature argument) training. training, retains 50 genes per SE (nfeature.per.se argument) model. Users can tune parameters accordingly. resulting W matrix can used infer SE abundances bulk gene expression profiles using DeconvoluteSE function. detailed documentation SE deconvolution available Inferring Spatial Ecotype Abundances Bulk Gene Expression Data.

title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Recovery from Single-Cell and Spatial Transcriptomics Data
text: Users can develop machine learning models enable spatial ecotype (SE) recovery held-single-cell datasets (e.g., scRNA-seq single-cell spatial transcriptomics). Spatial EcoTyper framework leverages non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) core algorithm SE recovery, given robustness demonstrated effectiveness recovering carcinoma ecotypes multimodal expression data prior study. , demonstrate train model recover SEs single-cell gene expression data.

title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Recovery from Single-Cell and Spatial Transcriptomics Data
text: tutorial, use Vizgen MERSCOPE data two samples demonstrate process developing cell-type-specific NMF models recovering SEs single-cell gene expression profiles, including single-cell spatial transcriptomics single-cell RNA-seq data. First load required packages vignette

text: Load gene expression matrix Load cell type SE annotations
code: ## Read the expression matrix for the first sample scdata <- fread("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=Melanoma1_subset_counts.tsv.gz",                  sep = "\t",header = TRUE, data.table = FALSE) rownames(scdata) <- scdata[, 1]  ## set the first column as row names scdata <- scdata[, -1]  ## drop the first column scdata <- NormalizeData(scdata)  ## Normalize the expression matrix  ## Read the expression matrix for the second sample scdata2 <- fread("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=CRC2_subset_counts.tsv.gz",                  sep = "\t",header = TRUE, data.table = FALSE) rownames(scdata2) <- scdata2[, 1]  ## set the first column as row names scdata2 <- scdata2[, -1]  ## drop the first column scdata2 <- NormalizeData(scdata2)  ## Normalize the expression matrix  ## Combine the two expression matrices genes <- intersect(rownames(scdata), rownames(scdata2)) scdata <- cbind(scdata[genes, ], scdata2[genes, ]) head(scdata[, 1:5]) ##          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3655 HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3657 ## PDK4                             0.000000                         4.221184 ## TNFRSF17                         0.000000                         0.000000 ## TNFRSF17                         0.000000                                0 ## ICAM3                            0.000000                                0 ## FAP                              0.000000                                0 ## GZMB                             0.000000                                0 ## TSC2                             0.000000                                0 ##          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3661 ## PDK4                                    0 ## TNFRSF17                                0 ## ICAM3                                   0 ## FAP                                     0 ## GZMB                                    0 ## TSC2                                    0 ## Load the single-cell meta data derived from MultiSpatialEcoTyper analysis scmeta <- read.table("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=MultiSE_metadata_final.tsv", sep = "\t",                       header = TRUE, row.names = 1) scmeta$SE[is.na(scmeta$SE)] = "nonSE" ## cells with NA predictions were assigned into the nonSE group  ## Matches the row names of meta data and column names of expression matrix cells <- intersect(colnames(scdata), rownames(scmeta)) scdata <- scdata[, cells] scmeta <- scmeta[cells, ] head(scmeta[, c("Sample", "CellType", "SE")]) ## title: NMF Model Development for Spatial Ecotype Recovery from Single-Cell and Spatial Transcriptomics Data
text: NMFGenerateWList function used train cell-type specific NMF models recovering SEs. Prior NMF, gene scaled mean 0 unit variance within cell type-specific expression matrix. satisfy non-negativity requirement NMF, cell type-specific expression matrices individually processed using posneg transformation, converts expression matrix two matrices, one containing positive values containing negative values sign inverted. two matrices subsequently concatenated produce training data. NMFGenerateWList function return list feature × SE matrices, representing cell type-specific gene signature matrix rows features columns SEs. default, cell type, NMFGenerateWList balances cell numbers across samples (via Sample argument) randomly subsampling sample target size equal median number cells per sample, downsamples total 2,500 cells across samples (downsample argument), selects top 300 variable features (nfeature argument) model training. recommend adjusting parameters based characteristics dataset available computational resources. quick demonstration, train model small example dataset using simplified, built-feature selection strategy. resulting W matrices used recover SEs spatial transcriptomics single-cell RNA-seq using RecoverSE function. detailed documentation SE recovery available Recovering Spatial Ecotypes Single-Cell RNA-seq Data Recovering Spatial Ecotypes Single-Cell Spatial Transcriptomics Data. Note: obtain robust model, recommend performing multiple runs constructing ensemble. analysis (Zhang et al.), repeated training 50 times averaged resulting basis matrices generate ensemble matrix W cell type. NMF model training can time-consuming. improve efficiency, recommend running training jobs Slurm array jobs allocating sufficient computational resources (e.g., CPU cores) task. example Slurm array script provided . nmf_trainings.R submit_slurm_jobs_nmf.sh jobs completed, resulting basis matrices can aggregated cell type using AggregateRecoverModels function. basis matrix, genes highest specific expression cell state identified. Genes consistently selected cell state across half repetitions retained final model default. final basis matrix generated averaging corresponding weights across repetitions.
code: # training Ws = NMFGenerateWList(scdata, scmeta,                        CellType = "CellType",                       SE = "SE", Sample = "Sample",                       nfeature = 300,                        nfeature.per.se = 50,                        ncores = 8) class(Ws) ## [1] "list" names(Ws) ## [1] "B"           "CD4T"        "CD8T"        "DC"          "Endothelial" ## [6] "Fibroblast"  "Macrophage"  "NK"          "Plasma" head(Ws[[2]]) ##                   SE7       SE3       SE4       SE8       SE6       SE1 ## FOXP3__pos  0.5004127 0.6673784 0.3470760 0.6797704 0.4872819 1.0338540 ## CXCR4__neg  0.2357855 0.6635520 0.2114708 1.0913564 0.3938264 0.6861448 ## ICAM3__neg  0.4751354 0.5266059 0.4683919 0.6175067 0.4831921 0.5026104 ## STAT3__neg  0.4748124 0.5139846 0.4922895 0.6149234 0.5547521 0.5395274 ## CCL5__neg   0.5197869 0.5246213 0.4483178 0.5640050 0.5111583 0.6468350 ## BCL2L1__pos 0.5060547 0.5936575 0.4228397 0.6190426 0.5072450 0.4724431 ##                   SE9       SE5       SE2 ## FOXP3__pos  0.5133462 0.5363018 0.7832876 ## CXCR4__neg  0.2967064 0.5645048 0.9790789 ## ICAM3__neg  0.4267446 0.6177860 0.8404026 ## STAT3__neg  0.5390224 0.5108202 0.7087075 ## CCL5__neg   0.5040774 0.5681576 0.6594520 ## BCL2L1__pos 0.6042824 0.5519758 0.6467089 # ============================================================ # Demo script for NMF training (do not run as-is without setup) # Designed for Slurm array jobs: one run per task # ============================================================  # ---- Parse command-line argument (task index) ---- args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)  if (length(args) < 1) {   stop("Usage: Rscript nmf_trainings.R <task_id>") }  i <- as.integer(args[1]) if (is.na(i)) {   stop("Error: <task_id> must be an integer.") }  message("Running NMF training for iteration: ", i)  # ---- Load required libraries and data (user-defined) ---- library(SpatialEcoTyper) # scdata <- ... # scmeta <- ...  # ---- Run NMF training ---- Ws <- NMFGenerateWList(   scdata, scmeta,   CellType = "CellType",   SE = "SE",   Sample = "Sample",   nfeature = 500,   nfeature.per.se = Inf,  # disable internal feature selection   ncores = 16 )  # ---- Save output ---- saveRDS(Ws, paste0("path/to/SE_recovery_nmf_models_", i, ".rds")) #!/bin/bash #SBATCH --job-name=nmf_train #SBATCH --array=1-50 #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=16 #SBATCH --mem=32G #SBATCH --time=24:00:00 #SBATCH --output=logs/nmf_%A_%a.out Rscript nmf_trainings.R $SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID files = list.files("path/to/", "SE_recovery_nmf_models_",                     full.names = TRUE) Ws_list = lapply(files, readRDS) Ws_final = AggregateRecoverModels(Ws_list)

title: Spatial ecotype analaysis
text: Spatial EcoTyper versatile framework identifying spatially distinct multicellular communities, termed spatial ecotypes, single-cell spatial transcriptomics data. addition, provides unified methods recovery spatial ecotypes across multiple data modalities, including spatial transcriptomics, single-cell RNA-seq, bulk transcriptomic datasets. Spatial EcoTyper available R package, comprehensive documentation accessible https://digitalcytometry.github.io/spatialecotyper, including eight tutorials illustrating key functionalities framework: Tutorial 1: Discovering Spatial Ecotypes Single Spatial Transcriptomics Sample Tutorial 2: Discovering Conserved Spatial Ecotypes Across Multiple Spatial Transcriptomics Samples Tutorial 3: NMF Model Development Spatial Ecotype Recovery Single-Cell Spatial Transcriptomics Data Tutorial 4: Identifying SE-Specific Cell States via Leave-One-Sample-Cross-Validation Tutorial 5: Recovering Spatial Ecotypes Single-Cell Spatial Transcriptomics Data Tutorial 6: Recovering Spatial Ecotypes Single-Cell RNA-seq Data Tutorial 7: NMF Model Development Spatial Ecotype Deconvolution Bulk Gene Expression Data Tutorial 8: Inferring Spatial Ecotype Abundances Bulk Gene Expression Data Spatial EcoTyper companion method Liquid EcoTyper available web application https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/. platform enables recovery spatial ecotypes single-cell RNA-seq single-cell spatial transcriptomics data, well inference spatial ecotype abundances Visium spatial transcriptomics, bulk RNA-seq, tumor plasma methylation profiles. Note: Spatial EcoTyper depends extensively Seurat key processes like dimensionality reduction, UMAP embedding, clustering, visualization. Initially developed using Seurat v4.3, tool thoroughly tested validated Seurat v5. Although UMAP embeddings clustering results show slight differences Seurat v4 v5, overall consistency remains strong, ensuring core biological insights preserved across versions.

title: Spatial ecotype analaysis

text: Spatial EcoTyper available R package. already Seurat installed, can install Spatial EcoTyper dependencies directly R: Create environment install core dependencies Install R packages ERROR: dependency ‘GetoptLong’ available package ‘ComplexHeatmap’ installation within R console fails, can try installing necessary packages via conda install mamba install. Failed install ‘presto’ GitHub: HTTP error 401. Bad credentials resolve issue, ’ll need authenticate using personal access token (PAT). can generate GitHub personal access token following GitHub’s documentation. generating token, set environment variable R using following code. Replace “YOUR_TOKEN” actual token: source code Spatial EcoTyper available https://github.com/digitalcytometry/spatialecotyper. downloading package, can install source code using command:
code: ## Install dependencies BiocManager::install(c("remotes", "NMF", "dplyr", "tidyr", "pals", "parallel",                         "data.table", "spdep", "ComplexHeatmap",                        "immunogenomics/presto"))  ## Install SpatialEcoTyper BiocManager::install("digitalcytometry/spatialecotyper") conda create -n spatialecotyper -y conda activate spatialecotyper conda install -y -c conda-forge r-base r-seurat r-spdep r-nmf conda install -y -c bioconda bioconductor-complexheatmap if(!"BiocManager" %in% installed.packages()){   install.packages("BiocManager") }  ## Install dependencies BiocManager::install("immunogenomics/presto")  ## Install SpatialEcoTyper BiocManager::install("digitalcytometry/spatialecotyper") ## Set the token in your R environment: Sys.setenv(GITHUB_PAT="YOUR_TOKEN")  ## Install the package from GitHub: BiocManager::install("immunogenomics/presto") install.packages("SpatialEcoTyper.tar.gz", repos = NULL)

what: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
text: Aggregate multiple recovery model weight matrices identifying consistently selected marker genes across models averaging weights.

title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
code: AggregateRecoverModels(   model_list,   delta.threshold = 0.01,   min.model.fraction = 0.5 )

title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
text: model_list list recovery model objects. element contain named list weight matrices indexed cell type, derived `NMFGenerateWList`. delta.threshold Minimum delta weights required gene considered associated spatial ecotype. Default `0.01`. min.model.fraction Minimum fraction models gene must selected retained. Default `0.5`.

title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
text: named list aggregated weight matrices, one per cell type.

title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels
text: function designed aggregating recovery models generated repeated training runs cross-validation folds. cell type, genes retained consistently selected across models based : minimum delta threshold required gene selected minimum fraction models gene selected Positive negative feature pairs (`__pos` `__neg`) retained selected genes. Final weights computed mean across non-missing values.

title: Aggregate recovery models across runs — AggregateRecoverModels

Two columns (X Y) spatial coordinates required. obj Seurat object returned SpatialEcoTyper function. col Character string specifying name column `obj@meta.data` containing spatial ecotype annotations. Default `"SE"`. dropcell Logical. TRUE, cells assigned spatial ecotype (outside radius) removed returned metadata. Default TRUE.

title: Compute co-association of cell states across samples — Coassociation
text: symmetric matrix Pearson correlation coefficients cell states, averaged across multiple normalization strategies.

title: Compute co-association of cell states across samples — Coassociation
text: Cell states defined concatenations SE cell type labels. function computes abundances SE-associated cell state four schemes: (1) including SE NonSE states, (2) excluding NonSE states, (3) (1), treating zero abundance missing values (NA), (4) (2), treating zero abundance NA. Pairwise Pearson correlations cell states calculated across scRNA-seq samples abundance matrix, using cor function R pairwise complete observations. final co-association values obtained averaging correlations across four schemes.

title: Evaluate Cell State Colocalization — Colocalization
code: Colocalization(   scmeta,   coords = c("X", "Y"),   SE = "SE",   CellType = "CellType",   radius = 50,   k = 200,   min.cell = 10,   nperm = 1000,   test = TRUE,   ncores = 16 )

title: Evaluate Cell State Colocalization — Colocalization
text: scmeta data.frame containing single-cell metadata, row represents cell. Row names correspond unique cell IDs. coords Character vector specifying column names scmeta spatial coordinates (e.g., c("X", "Y")). SE Character. Column name scmeta specifying spatial ecotype labels. CellType Character. Column name scmeta specifying cell type annotations. Permutations performed within cell type preserve cell-type composition. radius Numeric. Maximum Euclidean distance threshold (units X Y) defining neighboring cells. Default 50. k Integer. Number nearest neighbors queried per cell applying distance filter. Default 200. min.cell Integer. Minimum number neighboring cells required cell included analysis. Cells fewer neighbors excluded. Default 10. nperm Integer. Number permutations used generate null distribution. Default 1000. test Logical. Whether compute statistical significance (P-values). TRUE, Z-scores P-values returned; otherwise Z-scores returned. Default TRUE. ncores Integer. Number cores use parallel computation permutations. Default 16.

title: Evaluate Cell State Colocalization — Colocalization
text: test = TRUE, list containing: ColocIndex matrix Z-scored colocalization indices cell states Pval vector P-values assessing co-association significance test = FALSE, returns Z-score matrix colocalization indices.

title: Evaluate Cell State Colocalization — Colocalization
text: function performs following steps: Computes observed cell state colocalization using spatial neighbor relationships Generates null distribution permuting cell state labels within cell type Computes mean standard deviation permuted colocalization matrices Standardizes observed values Z-scores Optionally computes statistical significance using CoassociationTest approach preserves cell-type composition testing whether spatial organization cell states deviates random expectation.

Default 20. k integer specifying number nearest neighbors consider constructing KNN graph. Default 50. min.cts.per.region integer specifying minimum number distinct cell types must present spatial region retained analysis. Default 1. ncores integer specifying number cores use parallel computation. Default 1.

title: Construct Cell-Type-Specific Similarity Network — GetSNList
text: list sparse matrices matrix represents similarity network (SN) sample. rows columns matrix correspond spatial regions, values represent similarity scores.

title: Construct Cell-Type-Specific Similarity Network — GetSNList
text: function first trims input embeddings retain top `npcs` principal components features. filters spatial regions meet minimum cell type threshold. sample, function computes KNN graph using trimmed embeddings converts resulting distances similarity scores. Missing spatial regions filled zeros ensure samples regions.

what: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
title: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
text: function computes spatial metacells single-cell spatial transcriptomics. Spatial metacells represent aggregated expression profiles spatially proximal cells. function utilizes k-nearest neighbor (KNN) weighting aggregate expression profiles neighboring cells generate metacell expression profiles cell type.

title: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
code: GetSpatialMetacells(   normdata,   metadata,   X = "X",   Y = "Y",   CellType = "CellType",   spotCoord = NULL,   k = 20,   radius = 50,   min.cells.per.region = 1,   ncores = 4 )

title: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
text: normdata matrix data frame representing normalized gene expression data, rows correspond genes columns correspond cells. metadata data frame containing metadata associated cell. Must include spatial coordinates (e.g., X Y) well cell type annotations. row names metadata must match column names normdata. X Character string specifying column name metadata containing X spatial coordinates. Y Character string specifying column name metadata containing Y spatial coordinates. CellType Character string specifying column name metadata containing cell type annotations. spotCoord data frame containing spatial coordinates (X Y) spatial neighborhood, neighborhood identifiers row names. k Integer specifying number nearest spatial neighbors constructing metacells. radius Numeric value specifying radius (units spatial coordinates) within neighboring cells considered. min.cells.per.region Integer specifying minimum number cells required spatial neighborhood compute metacells. ncores Integer specifying number CPU cores use parallel processing (default: 4).

title: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
text: matrix containing spatial metacell expression profiles, rows representing individual genes         columns representing metacells.

title: Construct Spatial Metacells from Single-Cell Spatial Data — GetSpatialMetacells
code: library(data.table) library(Seurat) library(SpatialEcoTyper) scdata <- fread("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=Melanoma1_subset_counts.tsv.gz",                 sep = "\t",header = TRUE, data.table = FALSE) rownames(scdata) <- scdata[, 1] scdata <- as.matrix(scdata[, -1]) normdata = NormalizeData(scdata) scmeta <- read.table("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=Melanoma1_subset_scmeta.tsv",                       sep = "\t",header = TRUE, row.names = 1)  # Construct spatial metacells around B cells snmeta = scmeta[scmeta$CellType=="B", c("X", "Y")] metacells <- GetSpatialMetacells(normdata = normdata,                                  metadata = scmeta,                                  X = "X", Y = "Y",                                  CellType = "CellType",                                  spotCoord = snmeta) head(metacells) #> 6 x 9534 Matrix of class "dgeMatrix" #> title: Draw Heatmap — HeatmapView
text: Draw Heatmap

title: Draw Heatmap — HeatmapView
code: HeatmapView(   mat,   breaks = c(0, 0.6, 1.2),   colors = c("#ffffd9", "#edf8b1", "#225ea8"),   na_col = "grey",   name = "hmap",   cluster_rows = FALSE,   row_dend_side = c("left", "right"),   cluster_cols = FALSE,   column_dend_side = c("top", "bottom"),   show_row_names = TRUE,   row_names_side = "left",   row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12),   row_names_rot = 0,   show_column_names = TRUE,   column_names_side = "bottom",   column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12),   column_names_rot = 90,   show_legend = TRUE,   top_ann = NULL,   top_ann_col = NULL,   show_top_legend = show_legend,   bott_ann = NULL,   bott_ann_col = NULL,   show_bott_legend = show_legend,   left_ann = NULL,   left_ann_col = NULL,   show_left_legend = show_legend,   right_ann = NULL,   right_ann_col = NULL,   show_right_legend = show_legend,   show_ann_name = TRUE,   annotation_legend_param = list(),   row_split = NULL,   column_split = NULL,   show_heatmap_legend = TRUE,   legend_title = NULL,   legend_title_position = "lefttop",   legend_direction = "vertical",   legend_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12),   legend_labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12),   legend_height = 2,   legend_width = 0.3,   legend_side = "right",   ... )

title: Draw Heatmap — HeatmapView
text: mat Matrix like object. breaks vector indicating numeric breaks. colors vector colors correspond values breaks. na_col Color NA values. name Heatmap name. cluster_rows ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. row_dend_side ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. cluster_cols ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. column_dend_side ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. show_row_names ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. row_names_side ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. row_names_gp ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. row_names_rot ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. show_column_names ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. column_names_side ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. column_names_gp ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. column_names_rot ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. show_legend Whether show annotation legends. top_ann data frame. column treated simple annotation. data frame must column names. Can also HeatmapAnnotation-class object. top_ann_col list colors contain color mapping df. show_top_legend Whether show annotation legends. bott_ann top_ann. bott_ann_col list colors contain color mapping df. show_bott_legend Whether show annotation legends. left_ann top_ann. left_ann_col list colors contain color mapping df. show_left_legend Whether show annotation legends. right_ann top_ann. right_ann_col list colors contain color mapping df. show_right_legend Whether show annotation legends. show_ann_name Whether show annotation names. annotation_legend_param list contains parameters annotation legends. row_split vector data frame rows split. cluster_rows clustering object, split can single number indicating split dendrogram cutree. column_split row_split. show_heatmap_legend Whether show legends. legend_title Character specifyin legend title. legend_title_position Position title relative legend. topleft, topcenter, leftcenter-rot lefttop-rot vertical legend leftcenter, lefttop horizontal legend. legend_direction Vertical horizontal? legend_title_gp Graphic parameters title. legend_labels_gp Graphic parameters labels. legend_height Height whole legend body. used vertical continous legend. legend_width Width whole legend body. used horizontal continous legend. legend_side Side put heatmap legend ... parameters draw.

title: Draw Heatmap — HeatmapView
code: library(grid) library(SpatialEcoTyper) library(ComplexHeatmap) #> ======================================== #> ComplexHeatmap version 2.20.0 #> Bioconductor page: http://bioconductor.org/packages/ComplexHeatmap/ #> Github page: https://github.com/jokergoo/ComplexHeatmap #> Documentation: http://jokergoo.github.io/ComplexHeatmap-reference #>  #> If you use it in published research, please cite either one: #> - Gu, Z. title: Train SE Deconvolution Model — NMFGenerateW
text: function trains Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) model SE deconvolution based given spatial ecotype fractions gene expression matrix. Prior NMF, gene scaled mean 0 unit variance. satisfy non-negativity requirement NMF, expression matrix processed using posneg transformation, converts expression matrix two matrices, one containing positive values containing negative values sign inverted. two matrices subsequently concatenated produce training data.

title: Train SE Deconvolution Model — NMFGenerateW

title: Train SE Deconvolution Model — NMFGenerateW

Default TRUE. nfeature Integer specifying top variable features training models (default: 300). nfeature.per.se Integer specifying maximal number features select SE (default: 50). method character string specifying NMF method use. Default "brunet".

title: Train SE Deconvolution Model — NMFGenerateW
text: matrix containing basis NMF W matrix rows features columns SEs.

what: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE
title: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE
text: function can recover SEs scRNA-seq data single-cell spatial data assigning single cell SE NonSE.

title: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE
code: RecoverSE(   dat,   celltypes,   scale = TRUE,   ncell.per.run = 500,   Ws = NULL,   min.score = 0.6,   ncores = 8 )

title: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE
text: dat numeric (sparse) matrix gene expression data, single-cell spatial transcriptomics scRNA-seq data. celltypes Character vector specifying cell type annotations cells included gene expression `dat`. using default model, cell types including B, CD4T, CD8T, NK, Plasma, Macrophage, DC, Fibroblast, Endothelial expected. scale Logical indicating whether perform unit-variance normalization (default: TRUE). Change caution. ncell.per.run Integer specifying maximum number cells per NMF prediction run avoid memory issues. Ws list cell-type-specific W matrices used recover SE-specific cell states. element list named corresponding cell type contain W matrix NMF model. min.score numeric threshold (0-1) specifying minimum prediction score SE classification; cells lower scores assigned NonSE. ncores Integer specifying number CPU cores use parallel processing (default: 8).

title: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE
text: data frame five columns, including CID (cell id), CellType (cell type), InitSE (raw prediction), SE (processed prediction), PredScore (prediction probability assigned SE). InitSE records raw prediction cells assigned 11 init SEs discovered MERSCOPE data. Cells assigned "NonSE"  1) initially assigned two SEs lack SE-specific cell states, cell states specific SE conserve across cancers 2) prediction score min probability threshold (min.score<0.6) default.

title: Recovery of SEs Using Pretrained NMF Models — RecoverSE

title: Identify Spatial EcoTypes from Single-cell Spatial Data (A Single Sample) — SpatialEcoTyper
code: SpatialEcoTyper(   normdata,   metadata,   outprefix = "Denovo",   radius = 50,   resolution = 0.5,   nfeatures = 300,   min.cts.per.region = 2,   npcs = 20,   min.cells = 5,   min.features = 10,   iterations = 10,   minibatch = 5000,   ncores = 4,   grid.size = round(radius * 1.4),   filter.region.by.celltypes = NULL,   k = 20,   k.sn = 50,   dropcell = TRUE )

title: Identify Spatial EcoTypes from Single-cell Spatial Data (A Single Sample) — SpatialEcoTyper
text: normdata matrix representing normalized gene expression data, rows correspond genes columns correspond cells. metadata data frame containing metadata associated cell. Must include spatial coordinates (e.g., X Y) well cell type annotations. row names metadata must match column names normdata. outprefix Character string specifying prefix output file names. radius Numeric specifying radius (units spatial coordinates) defining spatial neighborhoods around cell. Default 50. resolution Numeric specifying resolution Louvain clustering (default: 0.5). nfeatures Integer specifying number top variable features (default: 300) used analysis. min.cts.per.region Integer specifying minimum number cell types required spatial neighborhood. npcs Integer specifying number principal components (PCs) (default: 20). min.cells Minimum number cells / spatial-meta-cells (default: 5)  expressing feature/gene. min.features Minimum number features (default: 10) detected cell / spatial-meta-cell. iterations Integer specifying number iterations (default: 10) SNF analysis. minibatch Integer specifying number columns process minibatch SNF analysis. Default 5000. option splits matrix smaller chunks (minibatch), thus reducing memory usage. ncores Integer specifying number CPU cores use parallel processing. grid.size Numeric specifying grid size spatial discretization coordinates. default, size determined specified radius using `round(radius * 1.4)`. Increasing grid.size downsample spatial neighborhoods reduce memory usage, may remove cells fall grid bins. filter.region..celltypes character vector specifying cell types include analysis. spatial neighborhoods contain least one specified cell types analyzed. NULL, spatial neighborhoods included. k Integer specifying number spatial nearest neighbors (default: 20) used construct spatial meta-cells. k.sn Integer specifying number nearest neighbors (default: 50) constructing similarity networks. dropcell Logical. TRUE, cells assigned spatial ecotype removed returned metadata. Default TRUE.

title: Identify Spatial EcoTypes from Single-cell Spatial Data (A Single Sample) — SpatialEcoTyper

Rows are genes, columns are cells. scdata <- fread("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=Melanoma1_subset_counts.tsv.gz",                 sep = "\t",header = TRUE, data.table = FALSE) rownames(scdata) <- scdata[, 1]  # set genes as row names scdata <- as.matrix(scdata[, -1]) normdata <- NormalizeData(scdata) head(normdata[, 1:5]) #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3655 HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3657 #> PDK4                             0.000000                         4.221184 #> TNFRSF17                         0.000000                         0.000000 #> ICAM3                            0.000000                         0.000000 #> FAP                              3.552438                         0.000000 #> GZMB                             0.000000                         0.000000 #> TSC2                             0.000000                         0.000000 #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3658 HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3660 #> PDK4                             3.208945                                0 #> TNFRSF17                         0.000000                                0 #> ICAM3                            0.000000                                0 #> FAP                              0.000000                                0 #> GZMB                             0.000000                                0 #> TSC2                             0.000000                                0 #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3661 #> PDK4                                    0 #> TNFRSF17                                0 #> ICAM3                                   0 #> FAP                                     0 #> GZMB                                    0 #> TSC2                                    0  # Load single-cell metadata. Three columns are required, including X, Y, and CellType. Others are optional. scmeta <- read.table("https://spatialecotyper.stanford.edu/inc/inc.public.vignettes.php?file=Melanoma1_subset_scmeta.tsv",                      sep = "\t",header = TRUE, row.names = 1) scmeta <- scmeta[colnames(scdata), ] # match the cell ids in scdata and scmeta head(scmeta) #>                                         X         Y   CellType CellTypeName #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3655 1894.706 -6367.766 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3657 1942.480 -6369.602 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3658 1963.007 -6374.026 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3660 1981.600 -6372.266 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3661 1742.939 -6374.851 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3663 1921.683 -6383.309 Fibroblast  Fibroblasts #>                                  Region Dist2Interface #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3655 Stroma      -883.1752 #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3657 Stroma      -894.8463 #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3658 Stroma      -904.1115 #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3660 Stroma      -907.8909 #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3661 Stroma      -874.2712 #> HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3663 Stroma      -903.6559  se_results <- SpatialEcoTyper(normdata, scmeta,                               outprefix = "Melanoma1_subset",                               radius = 50, ncores = 2) #> 2026-07-05 21:07:26.070938 Remove 88 genes expressed in fewer than 5 cells #> 2026-07-05 21:07:34.76433 Construct spatial meta cells for each cell type #> Total spatial neighborhoods: 2315; total spatial meta cells: 9853 #> 2026-07-05 21:07:35.280399 PCA for each cell type #> 2026-07-05 21:07:36.791148 Construct cell-type-specific similarity network #> Removing 182 spatial neighborhoods with fewer than 2 distinct cell types. #> 2026-07-05 21:07:37.646195 Similarity network fusion #> 2026-07-05 21:07:37.64763 Normalize networks ... #> 2026-07-05 21:07:37.749613 Calculate the local transition matrix ... #> 2026-07-05 21:07:47.173801 Perform the diffusion ... #> 	2026-07-05 21:07:47.174664 Iteration: 1 #> 	2026-07-05 21:07:49.944853 Iteration: 2 #> 	2026-07-05 21:08:01.751391 Iteration: 3 #> 	2026-07-05 21:08:19.727598 Iteration: 4 #> 	2026-07-05 21:08:36.0478 Iteration: 5 #> 	2026-07-05 21:08:52.730985 Iteration: 6 #> 	2026-07-05 21:09:07.435482 Iteration: 7 #> 	2026-07-05 21:09:25.060788 Iteration: 8 #> 	2026-07-05 21:09:37.160684 Iteration: 9 #> 	2026-07-05 21:09:50.31496 Iteration: 10 #> 2026-07-05 21:10:06.980876 Create Seurat object and perform clustering analysis #> Warning: Data is of class dgeMatrix. Coercing to dgCMatrix. normdata <- GetAssayData(tmpobj, layer = "data") # Z-score normalization znorm_data <- Znorm(normdata) #> Centering and scaling data matrix head(znorm_data[, 1:5]) #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3655 HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3657 #> PDK4                           -0.3542511                        3.0673518 #> TNFRSF17                       -0.1114269                       -0.1114269 #> ICAM3                          -0.4726938                       -0.4726938 #> FAP                             3.2406352                       -0.2911087 #> GZMB                           -0.2421502                       -0.2421502 #> TSC2                           -0.1322335                       -0.1322335 #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3658 HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3660 #> PDK4                            2.2468524                       -0.3542511 #> TNFRSF17                       -0.1114269                       -0.1114269 #> ICAM3                          -0.4726938                       -0.4726938 #> FAP                            -0.2911087                       -0.2911087 #> GZMB                           -0.2421502                       -0.2421502 #> TSC2                           -0.1322335                       -0.1322335 #>          HumanMelanomaPatient1__cell_3661 #> PDK4                           -0.3542511 #> TNFRSF17                       -0.1114269 #> ICAM3                          -0.4726938 #> FAP                            -0.2911087 #> GZMB                           -0.2421502 #> TSC2                           -0.1322335  # title: Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix — .colocalization
text: internal function computes cell state–level colocalization matrix based spatial proximity single cells. first constructs cell–cell neighborhood graph using k-nearest neighbors within specified radius, aggregates neighbor relationships quantify interactions cell states.

title: Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix — .colocalization
code: .colocalization(   scmeta,   coords = c("X", "Y"),   CellState = "CellState",   radius = 50,   k = 200,   min.cell = 10 )

title: Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix — .colocalization
text: scmeta data.frame containing single-cell metadata. row represents cell. Row names correspond unique cell IDs. coords Character vector specifying column names scmeta spatial coordinates (e.g., c("X", "Y")). CellState Character. Column name scmeta specifying cell state annotations (e.g., "SE1_CD4T"). radius Numeric. Maximum Euclidean distance threshold (units X Y) used define neighboring cells. Default 50. k Integer. Number nearest neighbors queried per cell applying distance filter. Default 200. min.cell Integer. Minimum number neighboring cells required cell retained analysis. Cells fewer neighbors excluded reduce noise. Default 10.

title: Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix — .colocalization
text: numeric matrix size (number cell states × number cell states), entry represents normalized colocalization index two cell states.

title: Compute Cell State Colocalization Matrix — .colocalization
text: function performs following steps: Identifies spatial neighbors using k-nearest neighbors within specified radius Constructs sparse binary cell–cell adjacency matrix Maps cells corresponding cell states Aggregates neighbor relationships compute cell state–cell state interactions Normalizes interactions cell cell state levels Cells fewer min.cell neighbors excluded ensure robustness colocalization estimates.

title: Draw Rectangular Annotations for Matching Row/Column Groups in a Heatmap — drawRectangleAnnotation
text: Unlike simpler implementations, function correctly handles cases annotation label appears multiple disjoint segments (e.g., clustering reordering). function performs following steps: Converts annotation vectors character format Identifies contiguous blocks identical labels using run-length encoding Maps block coordinates heatmap's normalized coordinate system Draws rectangles around row–column block pairs sharing label drawing performed within decorate_heatmap_body, heatmap must already drawn calling function.

title: Draw Rectangular Annotations for Matching Row/Column Groups in a Heatmap — drawRectangleAnnotation
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ComplexHeatmap)  mat <- matrix(rnorm(100), 10) row_ann <- rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5) col_ann <- rep(c("A", "B"), times = 5)  p1 = Heatmap(mat, name = "hmap") p1 = draw(p1) drawRectangleAnnotation(p1,   rows = row_ann,   columns = col_ann,   col = "black" ) } # }

title: Robust Clustering via NMF (non-negative matrix factorization) — nmfClustering
code: library(NMF) library(SpatialEcoTyper) mat <- matrix(rnorm(1000, 3), 20) mat[mat<0] = 0  ## Specify one rank result <- nmfClustering(mat = mat, ranks = 3, nrun.per.rank = 3) predict(result) #>  [1] 2 2 3 1 3 2 2 2 3 2 3 3 3 1 2 3 2 3 2 2 2 3 2 2 3 3 2 3 3 1 2 2 3 1 2 2 2 1 #> [39] 1 2 3 2 3 2 3 1 3 1 2 1 #> attr(,"what") #> [1] columns #> Levels: 1 2 3  ## Determine optimal ranks by testing multiple ranks result <- nmfClustering(mat = mat, ranks = 2:5, nrun.per.rank = 3) result$p  result$bestK #> [1] 3 predict(result$NMFfits[[paste0("K.", result$bestK)]]) #>  [1] 2 2 3 1 3 2 2 2 3 2 3 3 3 1 2 3 2 3 2 2 2 3 2 2 3 3 2 3 3 1 2 2 3 1 2 2 2 1 #> [39] 1 2 3 2 3 2 3 1 3 1 2 1 #> attr(,"what") #> [1] columns #> Levels: 1 2 3

title: Transform a sparse matrix to rank space (rank non-zeros in each column) — rank_sparse
code: library(Matrix) # Create a sample sparse matrix sparseMat <- rsparsematrix(5, 5, density = 0.5) # Apply the ranking function rankedSparseMat <- rank_sparse(sparseMat) print(rankedSparseMat) #> 5 x 5 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix" #>                          #> [1,] 0.4 .   0.2 .   0.6 #> [2,] 0.6 .   0.4 .   .   #> [3,] 0.2 .   .   0.4 0.2 #> [4,] .   0.2 0.6 .   .   #> [5,] .   .   .   0.2 0.4
